questions.question: Why are Himalayan rivers called perennial rivers?
questions.answer: Himalayan rivers are called perennial because they receive water from melting snow and rainfall throughout the year.

questions.question: Why do Himalayan rivers carry large amounts of silt?
questions.answer: They flow through young fold mountains and erode rocks continuously, carrying large quantities of silt.

questions.question: Why do rivers form meanders in plains?
questions.answer: Because the slope becomes gentle in plains, causing rivers to flow slowly and form curves called meanders.

questions.question: Why do peninsular rivers depend on rainfall?
questions.answer: Because they do not originate from glaciers and are mainly fed by monsoon rains.

questions.question: Why are Narmada and Tapi different from most peninsular rivers?
questions.answer: They flow westwards through rift valleys instead of flowing eastwards into the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Why is the Godavari called the Dakshin Ganga?
questions.answer: Because it is the largest river of Peninsular India and has a large drainage basin.

questions.question: Why is the Brahmaputra river prone to floods?
questions.answer: Because it carries large amounts of water and silt during heavy rainfall.

questions.question: Why are deltas fertile regions?
questions.answer: Because rivers deposit rich alluvial soil in delta regions.

questions.question: Why are lakes important for regulating river flow?
questions.answer: Lakes store excess water during heavy rains and release it gradually during dry periods.

questions.question: Why are river valleys suitable for settlements?
questions.answer: Because they provide fertile land and easy access to water.

questions.question: Name any two east flowing rivers of Peninsular India.
questions.answer: Godavari and Krishna.

questions.question: Name any two west flowing rivers of Peninsular India.
questions.answer: Narmada and Tapi.

questions.question: Where does the Brahmaputra enter India?
questions.answer: It enters India through Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.question: Name two tributaries of the Brahmaputra.
questions.answer: Dibang and Lohit.

questions.question: Name two tributaries of the Krishna river.
questions.answer: Tungabhadra and Bhima.

questions.question: What is an oxbow lake?
questions.answer: An oxbow lake is formed when a meander loop of a river gets cut off from the main river.

questions.question: What is a lagoon?
questions.answer: A lagoon is a shallow water body separated from the sea by sand bars.

questions.question: Give two examples of lagoons in India.
questions.answer: Chilika Lake and Pulicat Lake.

questions.question: Why are rivers important for irrigation?
questions.answer: Rivers provide water necessary for agriculture.

questions.question: Why are rivers important for hydroelectric power?
questions.answer: Flowing water from rivers can be used to generate electricity.

questions.question: What causes river pollution?
questions.answer: River pollution is caused by sewage, industrial waste and agricultural chemicals.

questions.question: What is the National River Conservation Plan?
questions.answer: It is a government programme to improve the water quality of rivers.

questions.question: Why are rivers important for transportation?
questions.answer: Rivers provide natural routes for transporting goods and people.

questions.question: Why is river conservation necessary?
questions.answer: Because rivers are essential sources of freshwater and support ecosystems.

questions.question: Why should river pollution be controlled?
questions.answer: To protect water resources, human health and aquatic life.
